As you head into today's session, the single most important variable to monitor is the crude oil price action and its immediate pass-through to short-term inflation breakevens. If energy prices continue to climb, the rates market will keep squeezing the front end, and the pricing discrepancy between rates and credit will become unsustainable.

Now, overlay the equity supply story. The end of the twenty-three-year contraction in US equity supply means that risk assets can no longer rely on a shrinking share count to mechanically boost earnings per share. If equity risk premiums have to widen to absorb new supply from mega-cap tech floats, equity volatility will spill over into credit spreads.
